text: The word 'passion' on college apps makes me sick. I don't feel passionate about anything academic. I go through the motions because it's expected. Where did my curiosity go? Did school kill it?
text_review: The word 'passion' on college apps makes me sick. I don't feel passionate about anything academic. I go through the motions because it's expected. Where did my curiosity go? Did school kill it?

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
indicator: low motivation / thoughts of giving up
text: My laptop wallpaper is a countdown to graduation. Not because I'm excited, but because it's the nearest finish line. I'm not running toward something; I'm running away from this. That's my motivation.
text_review: My laptop wallpaper is a countdown to graduation. Not because I'm excited, but because it's the nearest finish line. I'm not running toward something; I'm running away from this. That's my motivation.

dimension_description: Feelings of emotional and physical depletion caused by school demands
text: My little cousin asked if I remembered his name. I blanked. I’ve seen him three times this month and was always on my phone reviewing flashcards. He walked away looking confused. My own family feels like strangers because my head is somewhere else.
text_review: My little cousin asked if I remembered his name. I blanked. I’ve seen him three times this month and was always on my phone reviewing flashcards. He walked away looking confused. My own family feels like strangers because my head is somewhere else.

dimension: Cynicism toward the meaning of school
dimension_description: Loss of meaning, interest, and motivation regarding school and schoolwork
text: I used to love writing essays. Now, the prompt just looks like a wall. I don't want to think, I don't want to analyze. I write the minimum required words and stop. The process used to feel creative; now it's just a transaction.
text_review: I used to love writing essays. Now, the prompt just looks like a wall. I don't want to think, I don't want to analyze. I write the minimum required words and stop. The process used to feel creative; now it's just a transaction.
